_comment: Combustion (Asta) rules, consumed by combustionRuleEvaluator.js. A planet within the listed angular orb (degrees) of the Sun's longitude is considered combust — its significations are said to be 'burnt' by proximity to the Sun. Orb values are the commonly cited classical Parashari figures. Mercury and Venus traditionally get a tighter orb while retrograde, hence the *Retrograde overrides below.
excludedReason: The Sun cannot combust itself. Rahu/Ketu combustion is inconsistently treated across classical traditions, so it is intentionally left out of this foundation phase rather than asserting a disputed rule.
